summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that with SSRF_PROTECTION_ENABLED=False (the default), the ssrf_safe_get() function in core/utils/io.py fetches arbitrary internal-loopback URLs. The fake internal service received the server-initiated request and the proof token was returned in the response body, demonstrating data exfiltration from internal services.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause ssrf_safe_get() to fetch the internal-loopback URL of the fake internal service. The proof token embedded in the fake service's response should appear in the ssrf_safe_get() return value, confirming that internal data was exfiltrated through the SSRF.
actual_observed_result: Running the exploit confirmed: (1) ssrf_safe_get() successfully fetched http://127.0.0.1:{port}/internal/secret-data with SSRF_PROTECTION_ENABLED=False, (2) the fake internal service received the server-initiated request, (3) the proof token EVOHUNT_SSRF_* was present in the response body, and (4) with SSRF_PROTECTION_ENABLED=True the same URL was blocked with 'URL resolves to a reserved network address (block: 127.0.0.0/8)'.
verification_method: The exploit starts a fake internal HTTP server on a random loopback port, calls ssrf_safe_get() with the internal URL, checks that the fake server received a request from the server process, and verifies the proof token appears in the response. A second test confirms the SSRF protection works when explicitly enabled.
evidence_produced_by_run: ssrf_safe_get() returned HTTP 200 from the internal-loopback URL.
The fake internal service received exactly 1 GET request to /internal/secret-data from 127.0.0.1.
The response body contained the proof token EVOHUNT_SSRF_552f7f3991c02c93 confirming internal data was exfiltrated.
With SSRF_PROTECTION_ENABLED=True, validate_upload_url() raised InvalidUploadUrlError: 'URL resolves to a reserved network address (block: 127.0.0.0/8)'.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the develop branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
